sources.source_title: Executive Order 8802: Prohibition of Discrimination in the Defense Industry
sources.notes: National Archives milestone document page explaining that Executive Order 8802 banned discriminatory employment practices by federal agencies and defense contractors and established the Fair Employment Practices Committee.

sources.source_title: African Americans in World War II
sources.notes: Overview noting that Roosevelt issued Executive Order 8802 in response to pressure from the March on Washington Movement and that it banned discrimination in the defense industry.
Historical overview explaining that Executive Order 8802 applied to federally contracted defense industries and created an enforcement mechanism through the Fair Employment Practices Committee.

sources.source_title: Executive Order 9981: Desegregation of the Armed Forces
sources.notes: National Archives milestone document page describing the 1948 order abolishing segregation in the armed forces and directing integration.

sources.publisher: Harry S. Truman Presidential Library and Museum
sources.notes: Primary text of Executive Order 9981 stating that there shall be equality of treatment and opportunity in the armed services without regard to race, color, religion, or national origin.

sources.publisher: Harry S. Truman Presidential Library and Museum
sources.notes: Archival collection on Truman's decision to desegregate the armed forces and the implementation context around the order.
NLRB overview explaining that the NLRA protects workers' right to organize and engage in collective bargaining.
NLRB FAQ page stating that the NLRA grants employees the right to form or join unions and engage in protected concerted activity.

sources.source_title: Basic Guide to the National Labor Relations Act
sources.notes: NLRB guide explaining the Act's protections and unlawful employer discrimination tied to union activity.

sources.source_title: Fair Labor Standards Act of 1938: Maximum Struggle for a Minimum Wage
sources.notes: Department of Labor history page stating that the FLSA banned oppressive child labor and established a federal minimum wage and maximum workweek.

sources.source_title: History of Changes to the Minimum Wage Law
sources.notes: Department of Labor history page documenting the original wage floor and later changes under the FLSA.
Wage and Hour Division history page noting that the division was created with enactment of the FLSA in 1938.

metrics.metric_name: Initial federal minimum wage under the Fair Labor Standards Act
metrics.methodology_note: Department of Labor history states that the original Fair Labor Standards Act established a federal minimum wage of 25 cents per hour.

metrics.metric_name: Initial maximum workweek under the Fair Labor Standards Act
metrics.methodology_note: Department of Labor history states that the original Fair Labor Standards Act set a maximum workweek of 44 hours.

sources.notes: Opinion page for the 1971 decision holding that facially neutral employment practices that disproportionately exclude minority applicants and are not job related may violate Title VII.
Justia topic page summarizing Griggs as a key employment discrimination case involving disparate impact.
Later Supreme Court opinion discussing Griggs and reinforcing the rule against discriminatory employment tests lacking demonstrable job relevance.
EEOC statutory page for the Civil Rights Act of 1991, including expanded remedies such as compensatory and punitive damages in certain discrimination cases.
Original text page for the Civil Rights Act of 1991.

sources.source_title: Enforcement Guidance: Compensatory and Punitive Damages Available under sec 102 of the CRA of 1991
sources.notes: EEOC guidance describing the remedies made available under Section 102 of the Civil Rights Act of 1991.
